name: Ridgeline
description: Self-hosted read-it-later bookmarking tool for developers (Go + PostgreSQL + React)

name: Glanceboard
description: Homelab status and uptime dashboard

name: Homelab
description: Personal Kubernetes homelab cluster and infrastructure automation

name: Ridgeline CLI
description: Command-line client for Ridgeline to save and search bookmarks from the terminal (Go + Cobra)

name: pg-migrate
description: Lightweight PostgreSQL schema migration tool written in Go

name: s3-backup
description: CLI utility for incremental encrypted backups to S3-compatible object storage

name: go-worker-pool
description: Reusable bounded worker pool library for Go with context cancellation

name: go-lru-cache
description: Generic thread-safe LRU cache implementation for Go using generics

name: raft-kv
description: Toy distributed key-value store built on a from-scratch Raft consensus implementation

name: dotfiles
description: Personal shell, Neovim, and tmux configuration managed with GNU Stow

name: go-snippets
description: Collection of small Go reference snippets and code examples

name: homelab-k8s
description: Declarative manifests and Helm charts for the homelab Kubernetes cluster (GitOps)

name: VPS Infra
description: Terraform and Ansible config for the public VPS hosting reverse proxy and edge services

name: Ridgeline Web
description: React frontend for Ridgeline (Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind)

name: Ridgeline API
description: Go HTTP API and Postgres backend powering Ridgeline

name: Ridgeline Extension
description: Manifest v3 browser extension to save the current tab to Ridgeline

name: Glanceboard Agent
description: Go agent that runs health checks and reports status to the Glanceboard dashboard

name: Homelab Monitoring
description: Prometheus, Grafana, and Alertmanager configuration for the homelab cluster

name: Personal Blog
description: Static developer blog built with Hugo and deployed to the VPS

name: go-snippets-cli
description: Small CLI to browse and copy saved Go snippets from the terminal
